title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense · Runtime Lens: Production Defense
summary: The newest artifact replaces production without a route diff, removed URLs are discovered by readers, rollback requires rebuilding, caches preserve broken assets, ownership ends after deployment, and generated volume is called completion. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the production defense increment in production publication, migration, and…
objectives: Defend reader value, content authority, parser scope, route integrity, accessibility, security, performance, deployment, recovery, and residual risk together.
Diagnose broken builds, partial deploys, missing routes, bad redirects, stale caches, expired domains, and compromised content with named owners and communication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Production Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production publication, migration, and recovery dossier.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-production-defense — Defend reader value, content authority, parser scope, route integrity, accessibility, security, performance, deployment, recovery, and residual risk together.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_release_recovery_defense_2(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-production-defense-4nfw8o
# Competency: ssg-production-defense.
# Case 1385: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_production_defense_4nfw8o(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_4nfw8o = "4nfw8o-o8wfn4-1385"
    assert evidence_variant_4nfw8o
    evidence_axes_4nfw8o = (
        "reader4nfw8o",
        "source1385",
        "parseo8wfn4",
        "route4nfw8o1385",
        "artifact13854nfw8o",
        "recoveryo8wfn41385",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_4nfw8o)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Production Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 1628 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend reader value, content authority, parser scope, route integrity, accessibility, security, performance, deployment, recovery, and residual risk together.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense.
